title: Meta in Talks to Lease Computing Power to Anthropic in Potential $10 Billion Deal
summary: A deal would underline how scarce computing power is for artificial intelligence development, and could create a new business for Meta.

title: Xi Jinping of China Pitches ‘Openness’ in Push to Shape the Path of A.I.
summary: Xi Jinping said the development of artificial intelligence systems should be a “symphony of global collaboration.”

title: Someone Used A.I. to Write an Unauthorized Biography of Me. I Don’t Recommend Reading It.
summary: Someone used A.I. to write my biography. Thousands more of those books are polluting Amazon. Who is behind all this drivel?

title: The A.I. Trade Secrets War + Economists Say ‘We Must Act Now’ + HatGPT
summary: “I don’t know if the public understands this, but these companies hate each other.”

title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
summary: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.

title: White House Teleprompter Operator Bet on Trump Speeches, Kalshi Says
summary: A technical assistant to President Trump won around $100,000, according to the prediction market, which flagged the activity to the federal government.

title: Mary-Dell Chilton Dies at 87; Helped Create First Genetically Modified Plant
summary: In 1982, she led the research team that figured out how to genetically alter plants, a discovery that would eventually transform global agriculture.

title: Google Ordered to Give A.I. Rivals More Access on Android Smartphones
summary: The decision by European Union regulators is a response to fears that Google will use its vast Android user base to gain an edge in A.I.

title: The Quest for ‘Technological Sovereignty’ in Europe (and Why It’s So Hard)
summary: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.

title: Judge Dismisses Suit Accusing Apple of Failing to Limit Child Sex Abuse Material
summary: The lawsuit highlighted concerns that the privacy of Apple’s iCloud allows illegal material to be easily stored and shared on the service.

title: Twitter Turns 20: Two Decades of Memes, Movements and Hot Takes
summary: The first tweets from the public appeared on July 15, 2006, several months after a soft launch. It was rebranded as X after being acquired by Elon Musk.

title: Data Centers to Add Billions in Power Costs in 13 States
summary: A power auction conducted by a giant grid operator is expected to add $6.3 billion in additional charges to consumers and businesses because of electricity needs of data centers.

title: OpenAI Is Showing Kalshi’s World Cup Odds in ChatGPT
summary: The partnership, a first of its kind for the artificial intelligence company, will use the prediction market’s data to power some search results about the soccer tournament.

title: How a Teacher Revived Backyard Baseball
summary: Backyard Baseball, a favorite game for 1990s kids, had been off the market for years. But Lindsay Barnett was determined.

title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
summary: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.

title: Europe Takes Step Toward Possible Social Media Ban for Children
summary: After the release of a new report, the European Commission is considering changing the rules across the 27-nation bloc.

title: The eBay Scandal Gets a Thriller-Like Documentary
summary: A newly available documentary, ‘Whatever It Takes,’ examines eBay workers’ plot to cyberstalk journalists and critics.

title: Meta Removes A.I. Feature on Instagram After Days of Backlash
summary: Users and Hollywood agencies raised privacy and copyright concerns about the new tool, Muse Image.

title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets
summary: The two companies struck a deal in 2024 to offer A.I. services on Apple devices, but their partnership has soured.

title: How Terrorist Groups Are Using A.I. to Gain an Edge in Battle
summary: A.I. chatbots are not just a propaganda tool for violent extremists but are aiding in bomb construction and attack planning, new research finds.
